subject: Add GitLab remotes + push 13 missing submodule mirrors per 2-upstream policy
context: Constitutional rule (parent CLAUDE.md): 'Mirror policy applies recursively. Every vasic-digital submodule we own MUST be mirrored to the same set of upstreams Lava itself mirrors to.' SP-3a spec deviation 2026-04-30 reduced submodule scope to 2-upstream (GitHub + GitLab). Tracker-SDK + HTTP3 + Mdns already complied; the other 13 had only GitHub. After Seventh Law propagation (commit e5b213d) the 13 had pushed to GitHub only — this artifact closes the GitLab side.

discovery.issue: glab repo create rejected `Auth/Cache/...` with 'path already taken'
discovery.root_cause: GitLab normalizes paths to lowercase; repos already exist at gitlab.com/vasic-digital/auth, /cache, etc., even though GitHub uses Capitalized paths.
discovery.resolution: Add gitlab remote with lowercase URL; existing GitLab repos accept the push.

follow_ups: Submodule .git/config now records two remotes per repo (origin+gitlab); future submodule pin updates require pushing to both. Lava's scripts/sync-tracker-sdk-mirrors.sh is the canonical pattern; consider generalizing to a sync-all-submodule-mirrors.sh that loops the 16 submodules.
GitLab path is lowercase by GitLab normalization; GitHub remains capitalized. Documentation should note this asymmetry.
